articles.title: Lower Unemployment Rate Supports Longer Pause for Fed
articles.summary: January's jobs data, released on Wednesday, bolstered expectations that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady for longer than previously expected.

articles.title: U.S. Hiring Starts the Year at a Strong Pace
articles.summary: The unemployment rate fell to 4.3 percent and the economy added 130,000 jobs in January. The gains were powered, once again, by health care.

articles.title: Chicago Fed Puts October Unemployment Rate at 4.35%, Little Changed From Last Official Report
articles.summary: WASHINGTON (Reuters) -The U.S. unemployment rate remained stable at around 4.3% in October according to a Chicago Federal Reserve bank economic model that uses private data to estimate the jobless rate even in the absence of government statistics that have ...

articles.title: FTSE 100 live: Rate cut seen as jobs market weakens, oil below $60
articles.summary: The rate of unemployment has risen to its highest level in almost a decade outside the pandemic as employers continue to shed jobs. The increase to 5.1% makes it even more likely that the Bank of England will order an interest rate cut on Thursday.

articles.title: Unemployment hits 4-year high
articles.summary: The latest jobs report shows U.S. hiring slowed in November and employers shed jobs in October, providing a clearer view of the labor market after months of data disruptions. American employers added 64,000 jobs in November, after an estimated loss ...
